task_completion.description: OpenOffice Writer is open and contains the screenplay text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an OpenOffice Writer window displaying the text from the scene draft, including the scene heading 'INT. BRIDGE - DAY' and character names like 'CMDR. CHEN'.

task_completion.description: The required custom paragraph styles are created in OpenOffice Writer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Styles and Formatting' sidebar (usually on the right) or a style dialog box listing the newly created custom styles: 'Scene Heading', 'Action', 'Character', 'Dialogue', and 'Parenthetical'.

task_completion.description: The 'Scene Heading' and 'Action' styles are applied to the appropriate text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the paragraph style dropdown in the top formatting bar showing 'Scene Heading' or 'Action' when the cursor or selection is on the scene heading or an action line.

task_completion.description: The 'Character', 'Dialogue', and 'Parenthetical' styles are applied with correct relative indentation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for character names (e.g., 'CMDR. CHEN') indented further right than dialogue lines (e.g., 'Report!'). The paragraph style dropdown should show 'Character', 'Dialogue', or 'Parenthetical' when the cursor is on these lines.

task_completion.description: The document is saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the title bar of the OpenOffice Writer window; it should display 'Nebula_Frontier_Sc3.odt'.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenOffice Writer GUI to format the text.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the OpenOffice Writer interface (menus, sidebars, dialogs) rather than terminal commands generating the .odt file.

integrity.description: Indentation was achieved via paragraph styles, not manual spaces or tabs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of the 'Styles and Formatting' tools. If non-printing formatting marks are visible, there should not be multiple space dots or tab arrows before character names or dialogue to create the indentation.

integrity.description: The document contains the actual scene draft text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the document must match the provided scene draft (e.g., 'INT. BRIDGE - DAY', 'CMDR. CHEN', 'Shields are down'), not generic placeholder text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The screenplay contains character names: 'CMDR. CHEN', 'LT. SPARKS', 'SHIP COMPUTER (V.O.)'. The scene heading is 'INT. BRIDGE - DAY'. Dialogue snippets include 'Report!', 'Shields are down', and 'Reroute auxiliary power'. The required custom paragraph styles are 'Scene Heading', 'Action', 'Character', 'Dialogue', and 'Parenthetical'. The final file must be saved as 'Nebula_Frontier_Sc3.odt'.
